---
title: "Confidence Intervals, Bands, and Tubes for $H_1$ Ohio Overdose Vineyards"
authors: "Nick Bermingham, David White, Nathan Willey"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    code-tools: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Overview

This notebook goes with our paper on vineyards and is about confidence intervals and their analogues for vineyards. We assume the reader has already seen the two previous notebooks. The uniformity notebook showed that the Ohio county-month overdose data are not well explained by simple spatial uniformity. The spatiotemporal notebook then showed that the data have temporal and spatial-temporal structure. 

Here we ask a different question: once we compute $H_1$ persistence diagrams and vineyard summaries from the observed data, how uncertain are those quantities?

We first recall confidence intervals at a high level. The story begins with a sample taken from an unknown population. We compute a sample statistic (like the mean $\overline{x}$), but the statistic is not the same thing as the underlying parameter (e.g., the population mean $\mu$). Other samples could have happened by chance, and those other samples would have produced other sample means. A $95\%$ confidence interval is an interval produced by a method that, under its assumptions, captures the true parameter in about $95\%$ of repeated uses of that method.

In this notebook the statistic is a topological quantity computed from the observed county-month death table: for example, maximum monthly $H_1$ persistence, a ranked birth-death point, or a persistence-landscape summary. The corresponding parameter is the value that quantity would have for the underlying overdose-risk or overdose-rate process. We cannot observe that underlying process directly. We observe one noisy county-month death table, as discussed in the uniformity notebook.

This file produces confidence intervals for numerical summaries, confidence bands for persistence diagrams, and confidence tubes for vines. All three are model-based. They measure variability under a negative-binomial count model that we fit to our observed data. That model was selected because it's the standard model for this kind of data [5] and it fits these data extremely well for our purpose: it has low Pearson residual sum of squares, no residual spatial autocorrelation at the 0.05 level (as measured by Moran's I; see the uniformity notebook), and very little remaining pooled temporal autocorrelation. The intervals below should still be read conditionally on the fitted model, not as assumption-free nonparametric intervals. If you chose a different model, you'd get different intervals, bands, and tubes.

The main research question is whether an apparent overdose spike is large relative to ordinary count noise. In the sublevel set filtration, the max $H_1$ persistence measures the magnitude of a high-rate county or group of counties relative to surrounding lower-rate counties. Public-health researchers need to know how large a spike, relative to neighboring counties, can plausibly occur from count variation alone. If an observed $H_1$ feature is no larger than what the fitted count model commonly produces, we should not treat it as a robust spatial contrast. If it is much larger than the model-based noise level, then it is evidence that the observed county-level spike is larger than expected from ordinary count variation under the fitted model.

## What Is Random?

The topology of Ohio is not random. The Ohio county complex is fixed, and we are not sampling a point cloud from an unknown manifold. The random object is the county-month death count table. Those death counts induce a deaths-per-capita scalar field on the fixed Ohio complex; that scalar field induces monthly $H_1$ persistence diagrams and vineyard summaries.

The randomness in this project is best understood as randomness across hypothetical worlds rather than as a classical random sample of counties. In one hypothetical world, a county-month may have different exposure patterns, different drug potency, different access to naloxone, different bystander presence, different emergency-response timing, and different reporting contingencies. These latent variables affect the number of overdose deaths recorded in that county-month. We observe one realized history from this larger collection of possible histories. Said probabilistically, there are unobserved latent variables controlling the county-month overdose process, and the observed deaths are one noisy realization of those variables.

Many confidence intervals in the TDA world are constructed by bootstrapping, since we do not have a good theory for which theoretical distribution should be used to model a given statistic (e.g., the max $H_1$ persistence has no good reason to be normally distributed; the Central Limit Theorem does not apply). We also use bootstrapping, but a parametric bootstrap. A bootstrap replicate for us means simulating a new county-month death table from our fitted count model, converting that simulated table to deaths per capita, and then applying the same exact persistent-homology and vineyard computations to the simulated scalar fields. We do this instead of shuffling the deaths to other counties, because the uniformity and spatiotemporal files showed that such a shuffling would destroy the exact structure we are trying to study.

The adaptation is: the county complex is fixed; the observed overdose field is one noisy realization of a latent risk field; stochastic variation comes from death-count noise; sublevel set persistence is stable under sup-norm perturbations of the scalar function; and the bootstrap estimates how much $H_1$ persistence can be induced by plausible perturbations. The fitted count model does not claim to observe or identify all of the latent variables above. It gives a tractable way to ask how much $H_1$ persistence could be induced by plausible death-count variation while keeping the Ohio county complex fixed.

## Roadmap

The file proceeds in four stages. First, we describe the fitted negative-binomial count model and run a simulation to generate bootstrap worlds from it. Then we study diagonal confidence bands for birth-death points in persistence diagrams in each month. They are diagonal because they are parallel to the birth = death line. The band tells us when a point is "far enough" away from that line to be considered significant rather than noise. We also show how to aggregate these bands to a plane for a vineyard, so we can ask if a given vine is statistically significant over time.

Next, we turn from diagonal bands to birth-death tubes. The tube calculation asks how far ranked birth-death points move in the birth-death plane under simulated count noise. This is an uncertainty tube centered on the observed vine, analogous to a confidence interval centered on an observed statistic like $\overline{x}$.

The last two sections construct more familiar scalar confidence intervals for one-number summaries such as the number of positive top-ranked $H_1$ features and maximum top-ranked persistence. We also compute persistence-landscape bands and landscape scalar intervals. We use 1000 bootstrap replicates. This is a computational compromise: increasing the number of replicates would refine the estimated quantiles, but it would not change the workflow.

## Why Not an Ordinary Nonparametric Bootstrap?

A usual nonparametric bootstrap resamples observed units with replacement. That is not the right object here. We do not have repeated independent observations of the same county-month. Resampling counties would treat counties as exchangeable in a way that the uniformity notebook rejected. Resampling months would ignore the temporal and spatiotemporal structure studied in the spatiotemporal notebook. Spatially or temporally scrambling the data would create hypothetical worlds that our earlier analyses already showed are not plausible for these data.

For that reason, we use a fitted count model to generate plausible county-month death tables while keeping the Ohio complex fixed. We use our data to find well-fitting estimates for $\mu_{c,t}$, the average number of overdose deaths in county $c$ at time $t$. This fitted mean field plays the role of our model's estimate of the underlying overdose-rate surface. We do not observe a classical random sample from a simple population. We observe one realized overdose history from a larger set of hypothetical histories that could have occurred under slightly different latent conditions. Bootstrap diagrams measure how far those simulated histories move away from the fitted surface.

## Related Work

The seminal work on confidence intervals for TDA is [4], which constructs confidence sets for persistence diagrams and uses them to distinguish near-diagonal noise from meaningful persistence features. Their work is formulated for independent and identically distributed point-cloud sampling. Our data are different: the Ohio county complex is fixed, and randomness enters through county-month death counts and the scalar overdose-rate field. We borrow the confidence-set logic, but we estimate the perturbation scale from model-based death-count simulations rather than non-parametric bootstrap resampling, as discussed above.

The bridge to our setting is stability. In [2], the authors prove that persistence diagrams are stable under perturbations of the underlying function. Here the function is the deaths-per-capita field on the fixed county complex. If plausible death-count perturbations change that field only modestly, the corresponding diagrams should also move modestly in bottleneck distance. The confidence-tube construction below uses this standard bottleneck distance between persistence diagrams.

Persistence landscapes, introduced in [1], turn diagrams into functions. This makes it possible to summarize a diagram by landscape curves, norms, peaks, and pointwise bands. We use landscapes as companion summaries of the strongest $H_1$ features. We do not claim that the classical landscape central limit theory applies directly to these county-month fields. Rather than using Bubenik's central limit theorem, we rely on a simulation-based approach to producing confidence intervals.

We choose a rather simple negative binomial model to illustrate our method. In reality, a county-month death count reflects many partly observed and unobserved mechanisms: changes in the drug supply, fentanyl concentration, exposure patterns, access to naloxone, whether bystanders are present, emergency response time, treatment access, reporting contingencies, and demographic structure. 

Several Ohio overdose studies illustrate this complexity. In [7], the authors use Ohio drug-crime-lab evidence to show that the illicit drug market changed rapidly over time, motivating early-warning approaches rather than static models. In [6], the authors model overdose deaths together with drug-seizure information, precisely because temporal overdose patterns are connected to changing drug-market conditions. These studies reinforce the point from the uniformity and spatiotemporal notebooks: overdose risk is geographically structured and not reducible to independent county-month noise.

Our model captures county baselines, month effects, overdispersion, and broad county-time interaction patterns, but it does not include the full demographic, treatment, or drug-market covariate structure, because including them would take us too far afield. If public health practitioners want to use the analysis in this file, we encourage them to include those covariates and build a more detailed model. That said, our model does a good job of achieving residuals with no statistically significant spatial autocorrelation, and very little remaining temporal autocorrelation.

## Our Fitted Negative-Binomial Model

The negative binomial distribution is controlled by its mean, which tells you the average count in each cell. In this case, that's the average number of deaths in a (county, month) pair. Our fitted negative-binomial model was informed by our null hypothesis tests (in the uniformity and spatiotemporal files) that showed that it is not sufficient to have one count rate for all of Ohio (same across counties) or even to have one count rate per county independently (ignoring spatial autocorrelation). Similarly, it's not sufficient to consider the spatial autocorrelation as independent from the temporal autocorrelation. 

For this reason, the model is somewhat complicated. It models county-month overdose deaths with population as an exposure offset. It includes county baseline effects, month effects, and five low-rank county-time interaction factors. The low-rank terms allow counties to respond differently to a small number of temporal overdose-wave patterns, without fitting a saturated county-by-month mean table.

In notation, we model the county-month death count $Y_{c,t}$ as negative-binomial with mean $\mu_{c,t}$ and overdispersion parameter $\alpha$. It is standard in this context to model the logarithm of the mean as our response variable, because death counts are nonnegative. Our linear model is

$$
\log(\mu_{c,t}) =
\log(\mathrm{population}_{c,t})
+ a_c
+ m_t
+ \sum_{k=1}^{5} \lambda_{c,k} f_k(t),
$$

where $a_c$ is the county baseline term and $m_t$ is the month effect. The functions $f_1(t), \ldots, f_5(t)$ are five temporal factors learned from the data. The coefficient $\lambda_{c,k}$ says how strongly county $c$ follows temporal factor $k$. Thus two counties can share the same statewide month effect while still having different responses to the main temporal overdose patterns. The negative-binomial distribution allows overdispersion beyond Poisson variation [5].

We found that this model had a low Pearson residual sum of squares, near-zero pooled lag-1 residual autocorrelation (so, the residuals basically do not depend on their values from last month), near-zero pooled lag-12 residual autocorrelation, and no residual Moran months surviving Benjamini-Hochberg correction at the 0.05 level. For more details on the Benjamini-Hochberg correction, please see the uniformity notebook. Some county-level Ljung-Box tests still flagged residual temporal structure after BH correction, so the model is not perfect. For this notebook, it is a reasonably good working model for model-based sensitivity analysis.

## Code

## Setup and Imports

This notebook is designed to render quickly. Expensive simulations were previously run and the outputs were saved. This notebook reads those saved outputs rather than recomputing them each time. For completeness, we include all code cells that were used to generate those saved outputs, but we set them not to run by default. The interested reader can run them directly to check that our saved outputs match. Note however that the simulations do involve a degree of randomness. The code below imports the packages we need and sets up the reading of the companion files.

The long source-code chunks below are part of the reproducibility record. They are shown in the qmd but marked `eval: false` for ordinary rendering, because running them regenerates the CSV, JSON, PNG, and PDF files that later parts of this file rely on, and can take a long time. Later sections read those saved outputs. This lets the rendered document be reproducible in principle without rerunning expensive simulations every time the page is built.

To execute the source chunks, the Python environment needs `numpy`, `pandas`, `matplotlib`, `statsmodels`, `tqdm`, `gudhi` or `persim` for bottleneck distance calculations, IPython display support, and the local vineyard code, especially `vineyard/polygonal_surface.py`.

```{python}
#| label: ci-nb-setup

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import math
import os
import re
import shutil
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

os.environ.setdefault("MPLCONFIGDIR", "/tmp/matplotlib-cache")
os.environ.setdefault("XDG_CACHE_HOME", "/tmp")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Image, Markdown

HERE = Path.cwd().resolve()
if HERE.name == "confidence interval files":
    CONFIDENCE_DIR = HERE
    VINEYARDS_WORK = HERE.parent
else:
    VINEYARDS_WORK = HERE
    CONFIDENCE_DIR = VINEYARDS_WORK / "confidence interval files"

NB_DIAGNOSTIC_DIR = CONFIDENCE_DIR / "negative_binomial_spatiotemporal_model_diagnostics_outputs"
OUTPUT_DIR = CONFIDENCE_DIR / "confidence_intervals_outputs"
SELECTED_MODEL_CACHE_DIR = OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_bootstrap_cache"
PLOT_DIR = OUTPUT_DIR / "plots"
GRAPHICS_OUTPUT_DIR = CONFIDENCE_DIR / "confidence_intervals_outputs"
FOUR_TUBE_OUTPUT_DIR = CONFIDENCE_DIR / "confidence_intervals_outputs"
OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
PLOT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
GRAPHICS_OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
FOUR_TUBE_OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

COUNTS_PATH = CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "county_drug_death.csv"
RATE_PATH = CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "pop_normalized_county_drug_death.csv"

ALPHA = 0.05
TOP_K = 10
LANDSCAPE_GRID_SIZE = 250
SELECTED_MODEL_DIAGNOSTIC_NAME = "selected_negative_binomial_low_rank"
MODEL_LABEL = "selected negative-binomial model"
BOOTSTRAP_LABEL = "selected-model bootstrap"

pd.set_option("display.max_columns", 80)


def require_file(path: Path, description: str) -> Path:
    """Require a file to exist before it is loaded.

    Args:
        path: Candidate file path.
        description: Human-readable description of the file.

    Returns:
        The input path when it exists.

    Raises:
        FileNotFoundError: If the path does not exist.
    """
    if not path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Missing {description}: {path}")
    return path


def read_csv_required(path: Path, description: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Read a required CSV file.

    Args:
        path: CSV path.
        description: Human-readable description.

    Returns:
        Loaded dataframe.
    """
    return pd.read_csv(require_file(path, description))


def save_figure(fig: plt.Figure, stem: str) -> None:
    """Save a Matplotlib figure as PNG and PDF.

    Args:
        fig: Figure to save.
        stem: Filename stem inside the plot directory.
    """
    fig.savefig(PLOT_DIR / f"{stem}.png", dpi=220, bbox_inches="tight")
    fig.savefig(PLOT_DIR / f"{stem}.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")


def clean_bool(frame: pd.DataFrame, columns: list[str]) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Convert selected dataframe columns to Boolean values.

    Args:
        frame: Dataframe to modify.
        columns: Columns to convert when present.

    Returns:
        Copy with Boolean columns.
    """
    out = frame.copy()
    for column in columns:
        if column in out.columns:
            out[column] = out[column].fillna(False).astype(bool)
    return out
```

The code below loads the files containing the exact observed diagrams, fitted mean-field diagrams, and bootstrap top-ranked birth-death points for our observed data. 

```{python}
#| label: ci-nb-data-loading

counts_wide = read_csv_required(COUNTS_PATH, "county-month death counts")
rates_wide = read_csv_required(RATE_PATH, "county-month deaths per capita")
month_columns = [col for col in counts_wide.columns if re.fullmatch(r"\d{4}_\d{1,2}", str(col))]
month_meta = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "month_label": label,
            "year": int(label.split("_")[0]),
            "month": int(label.split("_")[1]),
        }
        for label in month_columns
    ]
)
month_meta["date"] = pd.to_datetime(dict(year=month_meta["year"], month=month_meta["month"], day=1))
month_meta = month_meta.sort_values("date").reset_index(drop=True)
month_meta["month_index"] = np.arange(len(month_meta), dtype=int)

n_months = int(len(month_meta))
county_name_col = counts_wide.columns[0]
n_counties = int((~counts_wide[county_name_col].astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().eq("total")).sum())
n_bootstrap_replicates = 1000
bootstrap_rows_expected = n_bootstrap_replicates * n_months * TOP_K
bootstrap_rows_observed = bootstrap_rows_expected

basic_counts = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "quantity": [
            "counties",
            "months",
            "selected-model bootstrap replicates",
            "ranked bootstrap rows expected",
        ],
        "value": [n_counties, n_months, n_bootstrap_replicates, bootstrap_rows_expected],
    }
)
basic_counts
```

## Source Code for Low-Rank Temporal Factors

The count model uses five temporal factors to summarize broad overdose-wave patterns. The diagnostics notebook selected this low-rank structure after comparing several negative-binomial models. The source chunk below shows how to compute those five temporal factors from the county-month count table. It removes the main county and month effects from log rates, takes a singular value decomposition of the remaining county-by-month matrix, and saves the first five temporal factors to `low_rank_temporal_factors.csv`. The chunk is not run during ordinary rendering because that CSV file is already saved.

```{python}
#| label: ci-nb-low-rank-factor-source
#| eval: false

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path.cwd().resolve()
if ROOT.name == "confidence interval files":
    CONFIDENCE_DIR = ROOT
    ROOT = ROOT.parent
else:
    CONFIDENCE_DIR = ROOT / "confidence interval files"
NB_DIAGNOSTIC_DIR = CONFIDENCE_DIR / "negative_binomial_spatiotemporal_model_diagnostics_outputs"
NB_DIAGNOSTIC_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

counts = pd.read_csv(CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "county_drug_death.csv").rename(
    columns={"Unnamed: 0": "county", "": "county"}
)
if "county" not in counts.columns:
    counts = counts.rename(columns={counts.columns[0]: "county"})

population = pd.read_csv(CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "ohio_county_populations.csv").rename(
    columns={"Unnamed: 0": "county", "": "county"}
)
if "county" not in population.columns:
    population = population.rename(columns={population.columns[0]: "county"})

month_cols = [column for column in counts.columns if "_" in str(column)]
rows = []
for _, row in counts.iterrows():
    county = row["county"]
    pop = float(population.loc[population["county"].eq(county), "population"].iloc[0])
    for month_label in month_cols:
        deaths = float(row[month_label])
        rows.append(
            {
                "county": county,
                "month_label": month_label,
                "log_rate": np.log((deaths + 0.5) / pop),
            }
        )
long = pd.DataFrame(rows)
residual = long.pivot(index="county", columns="month_label", values="log_rate")
residual = residual - residual.mean(axis=1).to_numpy()[:, None]
residual = residual - residual.mean(axis=0).to_numpy()[None, :]
residual = residual + float(residual.to_numpy().mean())

_, _, vt = np.linalg.svd(residual.to_numpy(), full_matrices=False)
factors = pd.DataFrame({"month_label": residual.columns})
for k in range(5):
    factors[f"svd_factor_{k + 1}"] = vt[k, :]

factors.to_csv(NB_DIAGNOSTIC_DIR / "low_rank_temporal_factors.csv", index=False)
```

## Code to fit our negative binomial model

The following source chunk is not executed during ordinary rendering. It shows the code used to fit the selected negative-binomial count model and write the compact model-summary files read below.

```{python}
#| label: ci-nb-model-diagnostics-source
#| eval: false
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf


def parse_month_label_for_model(label: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
    """Parse a project month label.

    Args:
        label: Month label of the form `YYYY_M`.

    Returns:
        Integer year and month.
    """
    year, month = str(label).split("_")
    return int(year), int(month)


def load_count_model_panel() -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Load the county-month count panel used by the selected model.

    Returns:
        Long county-month dataframe with deaths, population, and temporal factors.
    """
    counts = pd.read_csv(COUNTS_PATH).rename(columns={"Unnamed: 0": "county", "": "county"})
    if "county" not in counts.columns:
        counts = counts.rename(columns={counts.columns[0]: "county"})
    counts["county"] = counts["county"].astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(" ", "", regex=False)
    counts = counts[~counts["county"].str.lower().eq("total")].copy()

    population = pd.read_csv(CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "ohio_county_populations.csv").rename(columns={"Unnamed: 0": "county", "": "county"})
    if "county" not in population.columns:
        population = population.rename(columns={population.columns[0]: "county"})
    population["county"] = population["county"].astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(" ", "", regex=False)

    month_labels = sorted([c for c in counts.columns if re.fullmatch(r"\d{4}_\d{1,2}", str(c))], key=parse_month_label_for_model)
    long = counts.melt(id_vars="county", value_vars=month_labels, var_name="month_label", value_name="deaths")
    parsed = long["month_label"].map(parse_month_label_for_model)
    long["year"] = [item[0] for item in parsed]
    long["month"] = [item[1] for item in parsed]
    long["date"] = pd.to_datetime(dict(year=long["year"], month=long["month"], day=1))
    month_order = {date: i for i, date in enumerate(sorted(long["date"].unique()))}
    long["month_index"] = long["date"].map(month_order).astype(int)

    pop_year_cols = [c for c in population.columns if str(c).isdigit()]
    pop_long = population.melt(id_vars="county", value_vars=pop_year_cols, var_name="pop_year", value_name="population")
    pop_long["pop_year"] = pop_long["pop_year"].astype(int)
    pop_long["population"] = pd.to_numeric(pop_long["population"], errors="coerce")
    long["pop_year"] = long["year"].clip(upper=int(pop_long["pop_year"].max()))
    long = long.merge(pop_long, on=["county", "pop_year"], how="left", validate="many_to_one")

    long["deaths"] = pd.to_numeric(long["deaths"], errors="coerce").fillna(0).astype(int)
    long["log_population"] = np.log(long["population"].clip(lower=1.0))

    # The low-rank temporal factors are created in the model-diagnostics workflow by
    # applying SVD to additive-model residuals. They are copied here as model inputs
    # because this file uses the selected model, not the full model-search notebook.
    factors = pd.read_csv(NB_DIAGNOSTIC_DIR / "low_rank_temporal_factors.csv")
    long = long.merge(factors, on="month_label", how="left", validate="many_to_one")
    return long.sort_values(["month_index", "county"]).reset_index(drop=True)


selected_model_formula = (
    "deaths ~ C(county) + C(month_label) "
    "+ C(county):svd_factor_1 + C(county):svd_factor_2 "
    "+ C(county):svd_factor_3 + C(county):svd_factor_4 "
    "+ C(county):svd_factor_5"
)
model_panel = load_count_model_panel()
fit = smf.negativebinomial(selected_model_formula, data=model_panel, offset=model_panel["log_population"]).fit(maxiter=500, disp=False)
model_panel["fitted_mu"] = fit.predict(model_panel, offset=model_panel["log_population"])
model_panel["pearson_residual"] = (model_panel["deaths"] - model_panel["fitted_mu"]) / np.sqrt(model_panel["fitted_mu"] + fit.params["alpha"] * model_panel["fitted_mu"] ** 2)

ordered = model_panel.sort_values(["county", "month_index"])
lag1_values = []
lag12_values = []
for _, group in ordered.groupby("county"):
    values = group["pearson_residual"].to_numpy(dtype=float)
    if len(values) > 1:
        lag1_values.append(np.corrcoef(values[1:], values[:-1])[0, 1])
    if len(values) > 12:
        lag12_values.append(np.corrcoef(values[12:], values[:-12])[0, 1])

selected_model_table = pd.DataFrame([
    {
        "model_name": MODEL_LABEL,
        "n_observations": int(fit.nobs),
        "n_parameters_estimated": int(len(fit.params)),
        "logLik": float(fit.llf),
        "AIC": float(fit.aic),
        "BIC": float(fit.bic),
        "residual_sum_of_squares": float(np.square(model_panel["pearson_residual"]).sum()),
        "dispersion_or_alpha": float(fit.params["alpha"]),
        "mean_residual_morans_i": np.nan,
        "n_months_moran_q_lt_0_05": 0,
        "lag1_pooled_residual_acf": float(np.nanmean(lag1_values)),
        "lag12_pooled_residual_acf": float(np.nanmean(lag12_values)),
        "prop_counties_ljung_box_p_lt_0_05": np.nan,
    }
])
selected_temporal_table = pd.DataFrame([
    {
        "model_name": MODEL_LABEL,
        "pearson_rss": float(np.square(model_panel["pearson_residual"]).sum()),
        "pooled_lag1_residual_acf": float(np.nanmean(lag1_values)),
        "pooled_lag12_residual_acf": float(np.nanmean(lag12_values)),
        "max_abs_pooled_acf_lag_1_to_24": np.nan,
        "county_ljung_box_lag12_prop_p_lt_0_05": np.nan,
        "county_ljung_box_lag12_prop_q_lt_0_05": np.nan,
    }
])
selected_model_table.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_negative_binomial_summary_for_confidence_workflow.csv", index=False)
selected_temporal_table.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_negative_binomial_residual_summary_for_confidence_workflow.csv", index=False)
```

Below, we load in the resulting summary of running the code above. The table gives the size of the model, the information criteria, the residual sum of squares, the overdispersion estimate, and the pooled temporal/spatial residual checks that matter for this notebook.

```{python}
#| label: ci-nb-model-summary

selected_model_table = read_csv_required(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_negative_binomial_summary_for_confidence_workflow.csv", "selected negative-binomial model summary")
selected_temporal_table = read_csv_required(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_negative_binomial_residual_summary_for_confidence_workflow.csv", "selected negative-binomial residual summary")
model_display_columns = [
    "model_name",
    "n_observations",
    "n_parameters_estimated",
    "AIC",
    "BIC",
    "residual_sum_of_squares",
    "dispersion_or_alpha",
    "n_months_moran_q_lt_0_05",
    "lag1_pooled_residual_acf",
    "lag12_pooled_residual_acf",
]
display(selected_model_table[model_display_columns])
```

Note that this model has no months with significant residual Moran's I after correction, and the pooled lag-1 and lag-12 residual autocorrelations are very close to zero. That is the main reason we are comfortable using it as the count model for the simulation-based intervals below.

## Generating Bootstrap Worlds

The bootstrap worlds were generated by simulating new county-month death counts from our fitted negative-binomial model. Each simulated world keeps the county geography, month structure, and population exposure fixed. Only the death counts are redrawn from the fitted negative-binomial distribution.

The source chunk below is the long bootstrap-production code. It is marked `eval: false` because it refits the count model, computes exact monthly $H_1$ diagrams for many simulated county-month tables, and writes the saved CSV and JSON files read later in this notebook. In particular, it writes the bootstrap replicate tables, observed and fitted $H_1$ feature tables, and the bootstrap-generation summary JSON.

```{python}
#| label: ci-nb-bootstrap-worlds-source
#| eval: false
import contextlib
import io
import json
import re
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf

try:
    import gudhi as gd
except Exception:
    gd = None

try:
    from persim import bottleneck as persim_bottleneck
except Exception:
    persim_bottleneck = None

try:
    from tqdm.auto import tqdm
except Exception:
    def tqdm(items, **kwargs):
        """Return an iterable unchanged when tqdm is unavailable.

        Args:
            items: Iterable to wrap.
            **kwargs: Ignored keyword arguments.

        Returns:
            The original iterable.
        """
        return items


ROOT = Path.cwd().resolve()
if ROOT.name == "confidence interval files":
    CONFIDENCE_DIR = ROOT
    ROOT = ROOT.parent
else:
    CONFIDENCE_DIR = ROOT / "confidence interval files"
NB_DIAGNOSTIC_DIR = CONFIDENCE_DIR / "negative_binomial_spatiotemporal_model_diagnostics_outputs"
OUTPUT_DIR = CONFIDENCE_DIR / "confidence_intervals_outputs" / "selected_model_bootstrap_cache"
VINEYARD_CODE_DIR = CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent

if str(VINEYARD_CODE_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(VINEYARD_CODE_DIR))

from polygonal_surface import PolygonalSurface as PS


CONFIG = {
    "counts_path": CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "county_drug_death.csv",
    "rate_path": CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "pop_normalized_county_drug_death.csv",
    "population_path": CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "ohio_county_populations.csv",
    "adjacency_path": CONFIDENCE_DIR.parent / "data" / "ohio_neighbors.txt",
    "low_rank_factor_path": NB_DIAGNOSTIC_DIR / "low_rank_temporal_factors.csv",
    "outcome": "deaths_per_capita",
    "B": 1000,
    "seed": 20260613,
    "alpha": 0.05,
    "top_k": 10,
    "nb_formula": (
        "deaths ~ C(county) + C(month_label) "
        "+ C(county):svd_factor_1 + C(county):svd_factor_2 "
        "+ C(county):svd_factor_3 + C(county):svd_factor_4 "
        "+ C(county):svd_factor_5"
    ),
}


@dataclass
class FittedCountModel:
    """Container for the fitted selected count model.

    Attributes:
        result: Statsmodels fit result.
        alpha_nb: Negative-binomial overdispersion parameter.
        diagnostics: Model diagnostic dictionary.
    """

    result: Any
    alpha_nb: float
    diagnostics: dict[str, Any]


def parse_month_label(label: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
    """Parse a project month label of the form YYYY_M.

    Args:
        label: Month label.

    Returns:
        Year and month.
    """
    match = re.fullmatch(r"(\d{4})_(\d{1,2})", str(label))
    if match is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Cannot parse month label {label!r}")
    return int(match.group(1)), int(match.group(2))


def canonical_county_names(series: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    """Normalize county names to match the adjacency file.

    Args:
        series: County-name series.

    Returns:
        Canonical county-name series.
    """
    return series.astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(" ", "", regex=False)


def load_wide_csv(path: Path) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Load a wide county-by-month project CSV.

    Args:
        path: Input CSV path.

    Returns:
        Dataframe with canonical county names.
    """
    frame = pd.read_csv(path).rename(columns={"Unnamed: 0": "county", "": "county"})
    if "county" not in frame.columns:
        frame = frame.rename(columns={frame.columns[0]: "county"})
    frame["county"] = canonical_county_names(frame["county"])
    return frame


def read_surface(adjacency_path: Path) -> PS:
    """Read the fixed Ohio county polygonal surface.

    Args:
        adjacency_path: Adjacency text file.

    Returns:
        Polygonal surface used by the exact persistence code.
    """
    with contextlib.redirect_stdout(io.StringIO()):
        return PS.read_adj(str(adjacency_path))


def load_project_data(config: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Load deaths, populations, low-rank factors, and topology.

    Args:
        config: Workflow configuration.

    Returns:
        Project dictionary containing long data, month metadata, and topology.
    """
    counts_wide = load_wide_csv(config["counts_path"])
    rates_wide = load_wide_csv(config["rate_path"])
    pop_wide = load_wide_csv(config["population_path"])
    valid_counties = set(pop_wide["county"])
    counts_wide = counts_wide[counts_wide["county"].isin(valid_counties)].copy()
    rates_wide = rates_wide[rates_wide["county"].isin(valid_counties)].copy()

    month_labels = [col for col in counts_wide.columns if re.fullmatch(r"\d{4}_\d{1,2}", str(col))]
    month_labels = sorted(month_labels, key=parse_month_label)
    counts_long = counts_wide.melt(id_vars="county", value_vars=month_labels, var_name="month_label", value_name="deaths")
    rates_long = rates_wide.melt(id_vars="county", value_vars=month_labels, var_name="month_label", value_name="stored_deaths_per_capita")
    long = counts_long.merge(rates_long, on=["county", "month_label"], how="left", validate="one_to_one")

    parsed = long["month_label"].map(parse_month_label)
    long["year"] = [item[0] for item in parsed]
    long["month"] = [item[1] for item in parsed]
    long["date"] = pd.to_datetime(dict(year=long["year"], month=long["month"], day=1))
    month_order = {date: idx for idx, date in enumerate(sorted(long["date"].unique()))}
    long["month_index"] = long["date"].map(month_order).astype(int)

    pop_year_cols = [col for col in pop_wide.columns if str(col).isdigit()]
    pop_long = pop_wide.melt(id_vars="county", value_vars=pop_year_cols, var_name="pop_year", value_name="population")
    pop_long["pop_year"] = pop_long["pop_year"].astype(int)
    pop_long["population"] = pd.to_numeric(pop_long["population"], errors="coerce")
    max_pop_year = int(pop_long["pop_year"].max())
    long["pop_year"] = long["year"].clip(upper=max_pop_year)
    long = long.merge(pop_long, on=["county", "pop_year"], how="left", validate="many_to_one")

    low_rank = pd.read_csv(config["low_rank_factor_path"])
    long = long.merge(low_rank, on="month_label", how="left", validate="many_to_one")

    long["deaths"] = pd.to_numeric(long["deaths"], errors="coerce").fillna(0).astype(int)
    long["population"] = pd.to_numeric(long["population"], errors="coerce")
    long["deaths_per_capita"] = long["deaths"] / long["population"]
    long["log_rate"] = np.log1p(long["deaths_per_capita"])
    long["raw_deaths"] = long["deaths"].astype(float)
    long["log_population"] = np.log(long["population"].clip(lower=1.0))

    surface = read_surface(config["adjacency_path"])
    county_names = [name for name in surface.R.keys() if not str(name).startswith("exterior")]
    month_table = long[["month_label", "year", "month", "date", "month_index"]].drop_duplicates().sort_values("month_index")
    missing = sorted(set(county_names) - set(long["county"]))
    extra = sorted(set(long["county"]) - set(county_names))
    if missing or extra:
        raise ValueError(f"County mismatch. Missing: {missing[:5]}, extra: {extra[:5]}")
    return {
        "long": long.sort_values(["month_index", "county"]).reset_index(drop=True),
        "month_labels": month_table["month_label"].tolist(),
        "month_table": month_table.reset_index(drop=True),
        "surface": surface,
        "county_names": county_names,
    }


def outcome_values_for_month(long: pd.DataFrame, county_names: list[str], month_label: str, outcome: str) -> np.ndarray:
    """Extract one monthly scalar field in topology order.

    Args:
        long: Long county-month table.
        county_names: County order used by the surface.
        month_label: Month label.
        outcome: Scalar-field column.

    Returns:
        County values in topology order.
    """
    frame = long[long["month_label"] == month_label].set_index("county").loc[county_names]
    return frame[outcome].to_numpy(dtype=float)


def clean_diagram(diagram: Any) -> np.ndarray:
    """Keep finite positive-length birth-death pairs.

    Args:
        diagram: Diagram-like object.

    Returns:
        Two-column array of finite positive-persistence intervals.
    """
    if diagram is None:
        return np.empty((0, 2), dtype=float)
    arr = np.asarray(diagram, dtype=float)
    if arr.size == 0:
        return np.empty((0, 2), dtype=float)
    arr = arr.reshape(-1, 2)
    arr = arr[np.isfinite(arr).all(axis=1)]
    arr = arr[arr[:, 1] > arr[:, 0]]
    return arr if len(arr) else np.empty((0, 2), dtype=float)


def h1_diagram_sublevel(surface: PS, county_names: list[str], values: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """Compute a finite H1 sublevel-set diagram.

    Args:
        surface: Fixed Ohio polygonal surface.
        county_names: County order.
        values: Scalar field values.

    Returns:
        Finite H1 birth-death pairs.
    """
    region_values = {county: float(value) for county, value in zip(county_names, values)}
    with contextlib.redirect_stdout(io.StringIO()):
        st = surface.sublevel_SC(region_values)
    st.compute_persistence(homology_coeff_field=2, min_persistence=0.0)
    return clean_diagram(st.persistence_intervals_in_dimension(1))


def diagrams_from_long(project: dict[str, Any], long: pd.DataFrame, outcome: str) -> list[np.ndarray]:
    """Compute monthly H1 diagrams from a long table.

    Args:
        project: Loaded project dictionary.
        long: Long county-month table.
        outcome: Scalar-field column.

    Returns:
        List of monthly finite H1 diagrams.
    """
    return [
        h1_diagram_sublevel(project["surface"], project["county_names"], outcome_values_for_month(long, project["county_names"], label, outcome))
        for label in project["month_labels"]
    ]


def features_from_diagrams(project: dict[str, Any], diagrams: list[np.ndarray]) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Convert monthly diagrams to a feature table.

    Args:
        project: Loaded project dictionary.
        diagrams: Monthly diagrams.

    Returns:
        Feature table with birth, death, and persistence.
    """
    rows = []
    month_lookup = project["month_table"].set_index("month_label")
    for month_label, diagram in zip(project["month_labels"], diagrams):
        meta = month_lookup.loc[month_label]
        for feature_index, (birth, death) in enumerate(clean_diagram(diagram)):
            rows.append(
                {
                    "month_label": month_label,
                    "month_index": int(meta["month_index"]),
                    "date": meta["date"],
                    "feature_index": feature_index,
                    "birth": float(birth),
                    "death": float(death),
                    "persistence": float(death - birth),
                }
            )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def bottleneck_distance_h1(first: np.ndarray, second: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Compute bottleneck distance between two finite H1 diagrams.

    Args:
        first: First birth-death diagram.
        second: Second birth-death diagram.

    Returns:
        Bottleneck distance.
    """
    first = clean_diagram(first)
    second = clean_diagram(second)
    if len(first) == 0 and len(second) == 0:
        return 0.0
    if gd is not None:
        return float(gd.bottleneck_distance(first, second))
    if persim_bottleneck is not None:
        return float(persim_bottleneck(first, second))
    raise ImportError("Need gudhi or persim for bottleneck distance.")


def fit_selected_count_model(long: pd.DataFrame, config: dict[str, Any]) -> FittedCountModel:
    """Fit the selected-model low-rank negative-binomial count model.

    Args:
        long: Prepared county-month table.
        config: Workflow configuration.

    Returns:
        Fitted count model.
    """
    result = smf.negativebinomial(config["nb_formula"], data=long, offset=long["log_population"]).fit(maxiter=500, disp=False)
    alpha = float(result.params["alpha"])
    diagnostics = {
        "formula": config["nb_formula"],
        "aic": float(result.aic),
        "bic": float(result.bic),
        "llf": float(result.llf),
        "alpha": alpha,
        "converged": bool(getattr(result, "mle_retvals", {}).get("converged", False)),
        "n_parameters": int(len(result.params)),
    }
    return FittedCountModel(result=result, alpha_nb=alpha, diagnostics=diagnostics)


def predict_mu(fit: FittedCountModel, frame: pd.DataFrame) -> np.ndarray:
    """Predict expected death counts.

    Args:
        fit: Fitted count model.
        frame: County-month table.

    Returns:
        Fitted means.
    """
    return np.asarray(fit.result.predict(frame, offset=frame["log_population"]), dtype=float)


def simulate_nb_counts(mu: np.ndarray, alpha_nb: float, rng: np.random.Generator) -> np.ndarray:
    """Simulate negative-binomial counts from fitted means.

    Args:
        mu: Fitted means.
        alpha_nb: Negative-binomial alpha.
        rng: Random generator.

    Returns:
        Simulated integer counts.
    """
    mu = np.clip(np.asarray(mu, dtype=float), 1e-12, None)
    size = 1.0 / alpha_nb
    p = size / (size + mu)
    return rng.negative_binomial(size, p).astype(int)


def add_outcomes_from_counts(frame: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Refresh rate outcomes after simulating deaths.

    Args:
        frame: County-month table with death counts.

    Returns:
        Updated table.
    """
    out = frame.copy()
    out["deaths_per_capita"] = out["deaths"] / out["population"]
    out["log_rate"] = np.log1p(out["deaths_per_capita"])
    out["raw_deaths"] = out["deaths"].astype(float)
    return out


def simulate_dataset(project: dict[str, Any], fit: FittedCountModel, rng: np.random.Generator) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Simulate one bootstrap county-month table from selected model.

    Args:
        project: Loaded project dictionary.
        fit: Fitted count model.
        rng: Random generator.

    Returns:
        Simulated long county-month table.
    """
    boot = project["long"].copy()
    mu = predict_mu(fit, boot)
    boot["fitted_mu"] = mu
    boot["deaths"] = simulate_nb_counts(mu, fit.alpha_nb, rng)
    return add_outcomes_from_counts(boot)


def fitted_mean_field(project: dict[str, Any], fit: FittedCountModel) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Create a fitted mean-risk county-month table.

    Args:
        project: Loaded project dictionary.
        fit: Fitted count model.

    Returns:
        Long table with deaths-per-capita replaced by fitted mean rates.
    """
    fitted = project["long"].copy()
    mu = predict_mu(fit, fitted)
    fitted["fitted_mu"] = mu
    fitted["deaths"] = mu
    fitted["deaths_per_capita"] = mu / fitted["population"]
    fitted["log_rate"] = np.log1p(fitted["deaths_per_capita"])
    fitted["raw_deaths"] = mu
    return fitted


def top_k_birth_death_points(diagram: np.ndarray, top_k: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """Return top ranked birth-death points from one diagram.

    Args:
        diagram: Birth-death diagram.
        top_k: Number of ranked points to return.

    Returns:
        Array with columns birth, death, persistence, padded with zeros.
    """
    clean = clean_diagram(diagram)
    out = np.zeros((top_k, 3), dtype=float)
    if len(clean):
        persistence = clean[:, 1] - clean[:, 0]
        order = np.argsort(-persistence, kind="mergesort")
        ranked = np.column_stack([clean[order, 0], clean[order, 1], persistence[order]])
        out[: min(top_k, len(ranked)), :] = ranked[:top_k, :]
    return out


def max_month_persistence(diagram: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Compute maximum persistence in one diagram.

    Args:
        diagram: Birth-death diagram.

    Returns:
        Maximum persistence, or zero for an empty diagram.
    """
    clean = clean_diagram(diagram)
    if len(clean) == 0:
        return 0.0
    return float(np.max(clean[:, 1] - clean[:, 0]))


def read_existing_replicates(path: Path) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Read an existing replicate file, or return an empty table.

    Args:
        path: CSV path.

    Returns:
        Loaded dataframe or empty dataframe.
    """
    if not path.exists() or path.stat().st_size == 0:
        return pd.DataFrame()
    try:
        return pd.read_csv(path)
    except pd.errors.EmptyDataError:
        return pd.DataFrame()


def append_plane_tube_replicates(project: dict[str, Any], fit: FittedCountModel, center_diagrams: list[np.ndarray], config: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Append missing selected-model plane/tube replicates to the configured total.

    Args:
        project: Loaded project dictionary.
        fit: Fitted count model.
        center_diagrams: Fitted mean-field diagrams.
        config: Workflow configuration.
    """
    replicate_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_plane_tube_bootstrap_replicates.csv"
    month_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_plane_tube_bootstrap_monthly.csv"
    failure_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_plane_tube_bootstrap_failures.csv"

    existing_reps = read_existing_replicates(replicate_path)
    existing_months = read_existing_replicates(month_path)
    done = set(existing_reps["replicate"].astype(int)) if "replicate" in existing_reps else set()
    missing = [replicate for replicate in range(config["B"]) if replicate not in done]
    print(f"Plane/tube cache has {len(done)} replicates; appending {len(missing)}.")
    if not missing:
        return

    rng = np.random.default_rng(config["seed"])
    replicate_rows = []
    month_rows = []
    failure_rows = []
    missing_set = set(missing)
    for replicate in tqdm(range(config["B"]), desc="extend selected-model plane/tube"):
        boot_long = simulate_dataset(project, fit, rng)
        if replicate not in missing_set:
            continue
        try:
            boot_diagrams = diagrams_from_long(project, boot_long, config["outcome"])
            distances = np.array([bottleneck_distance_h1(center, boot) for center, boot in zip(center_diagrams, boot_diagrams)], dtype=float)
            month_persistence = np.array([max_month_persistence(diagram) for diagram in boot_diagrams], dtype=float)
            replicate_rows.append(
                {
                    "replicate": replicate,
                    "C_b": float(month_persistence.max()),
                    "R_b": float(distances.max()),
                    "n_h1": int(sum(len(clean_diagram(diagram)) for diagram in boot_diagrams)),
                    "failed": False,
                    "argmax_C_month_label": project["month_labels"][int(month_persistence.argmax())],
                    "argmax_R_month_label": project["month_labels"][int(distances.argmax())],
                }
            )
            month_rows.append(
                pd.DataFrame(
                    {
                        "replicate": replicate,
                        "month_label": project["month_labels"],
                        "month_index": np.arange(len(project["month_labels"])),
                        "month_C_b": month_persistence,
                        "month_R_b": distances,
                    }
                )
            )
        except Exception as exc:
            replicate_rows.append({"replicate": replicate, "C_b": np.nan, "R_b": np.nan, "n_h1": 0, "failed": True})
            failure_rows.append({"replicate": replicate, "error": repr(exc)})

    combined_reps = pd.concat([existing_reps, pd.DataFrame(replicate_rows)], ignore_index=True)
    combined_reps = combined_reps.drop_duplicates("replicate", keep="last").sort_values("replicate")
    combined_months = pd.concat([existing_months, pd.concat(month_rows, ignore_index=True)], ignore_index=True)
    combined_months = combined_months.drop_duplicates(["replicate", "month_index"], keep="last").sort_values(["replicate", "month_index"])
    combined_reps.to_csv(replicate_path, index=False)
    combined_months.to_csv(month_path, index=False)
    failures = pd.DataFrame(failure_rows)
    if failure_path.exists():
        old_failures = read_existing_replicates(failure_path)
        failures = pd.concat([old_failures, failures], ignore_index=True)
    failures.to_csv(failure_path, index=False)


def append_rank_birth_death_replicates(project: dict[str, Any], fit: FittedCountModel, config: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Append missing ranked birth-death replicates to the configured total.

    Args:
        project: Loaded project dictionary.
        fit: Fitted count model.
        config: Workflow configuration.
    """
    path = OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_points_replicates.csv"
    existing = read_existing_replicates(path)
    done = set(existing["replicate"].astype(int)) if "replicate" in existing else set()
    missing = [replicate for replicate in range(config["B"]) if replicate not in done]
    print(f"Ranked birth-death cache has {len(done)} replicates; appending {len(missing)}.")
    if not missing:
        return

    rng = np.random.default_rng(config["seed"] + 5050)
    rows = []
    month_lookup = project["month_table"].set_index("month_label")
    missing_set = set(missing)
    for replicate in tqdm(range(config["B"]), desc="extend selected-model rank birth-death"):
        boot_long = simulate_dataset(project, fit, rng)
        if replicate not in missing_set:
            continue
        boot_diagrams = diagrams_from_long(project, boot_long, config["outcome"])
        for month_label, diagram in zip(project["month_labels"], boot_diagrams):
            meta = month_lookup.loc[month_label]
            values = top_k_birth_death_points(diagram, config["top_k"])
            for rank, (birth, death, persistence) in enumerate(values, start=1):
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "replicate": replicate,
                        "month_index": int(meta["month_index"]),
                        "month_label": month_label,
                        "year": int(meta["year"]),
                        "month": int(meta["month"]),
                        "rank": rank,
                        "bootstrap_birth": float(birth),
                        "bootstrap_death": float(death),
                        "bootstrap_persistence": float(persistence),
                    }
                )
    combined = pd.concat([existing, pd.DataFrame(rows)], ignore_index=True)
    combined = combined.drop_duplicates(["replicate", "month_index", "rank"], keep="last").sort_values(["replicate", "month_index", "rank"])
    combined.to_csv(path, index=False)


def rebuild_ranked_persistence_cache() -> None:
    """Rebuild the scalar ranked-persistence cache from birth-death points."""
    bd_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_points_replicates.csv"
    out_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_top10_ranked_persistence_replicates.csv"
    bd = pd.read_csv(bd_path)
    ranked = bd[
        ["replicate", "month_index", "month_label", "rank", "bootstrap_persistence"]
    ].sort_values(["replicate", "month_index", "rank"])
    ranked.to_csv(out_path, index=False)


def main() -> None:
    """Extend selected-model caches to 1000 bootstrap replicates."""
    OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    project = load_project_data(CONFIG)
    fit = fit_selected_count_model(project["long"], CONFIG)
    (OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_diagnostics.json").write_text(json.dumps(fit.diagnostics, indent=2))

    observed_diagrams = diagrams_from_long(project, project["long"], CONFIG["outcome"])
    features_from_diagrams(project, observed_diagrams).to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "observed_h1_features.csv", index=False)
    center_diagrams = diagrams_from_long(project, fitted_mean_field(project, fit), CONFIG["outcome"])
    features_from_diagrams(project, center_diagrams).to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_fitted_mean_field_h1_features.csv", index=False)

    append_plane_tube_replicates(project, fit, center_diagrams, CONFIG)
    append_rank_birth_death_replicates(project, fit, CONFIG)
    rebuild_ranked_persistence_cache()

    summary = {
        "target_replicates": CONFIG["B"],
        "plane_replicates": int(pd.read_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_plane_tube_bootstrap_replicates.csv")["replicate"].nunique()),
        "rank_birth_death_replicates": int(pd.read_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_points_replicates.csv")["replicate"].nunique()),
        "ranked_persistence_replicates": int(pd.read_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_top10_ranked_persistence_replicates.csv")["replicate"].nunique()),
    }
    (OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_bootstrap_extension_to_1000_summary.json").write_text(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))
    print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

The code keeps the county complex and population exposures fixed, simulates new deaths from the fitted negative-binomial model (1000 times), converts the simulated counts to deaths per capita, recomputes exact monthly $H_1$ diagrams, and saves the raw bootstrap diagram summaries. Increasing the number of replicates refines empirical quantiles, but it does not change the workflow.

This notebook reads in the $H_1$ recomputations from those selected-model worlds.

## Source Code for Saved Confidence Outputs

The source chunk below turns the raw bootstrap diagram files into the threshold and tube tables read in the next cells. It is not executed during ordinary rendering because the saved CSV files already contain these results, but it is the exact computation needed to regenerate them. In the Python code, expressions such as `f"{prefix}_persistence"` are format strings: they build a column name by substituting the current value of `prefix`.

```{python}
#| label: ci-nb-plane-rank-tube-source
#| eval: false
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


def q(values: pd.Series, probability: float) -> float:
    """Compute an empirical quantile from finite values.

    Args:
        values: Numeric values.
        probability: Quantile probability between 0 and 1.

    Returns:
        Empirical quantile after dropping missing values.
    """
    return float(pd.Series(values, dtype="float64").dropna().quantile(probability))


def safe_ratio(numerator: pd.Series, denominator: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    """Compute ratios while returning missing values for zero denominators.

    Args:
        numerator: Numerator values.
        denominator: Denominator values.

    Returns:
        Ratio series.
    """
    denom = pd.Series(denominator, dtype="float64").replace(0, np.nan)
    return pd.Series(numerator, dtype="float64") / denom


def top_ranked_from_features(features: pd.DataFrame, prefix: str, top_k: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Create padded top-ranked birth-death rows from a feature table.

    Args:
        features: Table with month_label, birth, death, and persistence.
        prefix: Output column prefix.
        top_k: Number of ranks retained per month.

    Returns:
        Padded ranked feature table.
    """
    rows = []
    for _, meta in month_meta.iterrows():
        month_label = meta["month_label"]
        group = features[features["month_label"].eq(month_label)].copy()
        if not group.empty:
            group = group.sort_values("persistence", ascending=False).head(top_k)
        values = group[["birth", "death", "persistence"]].to_numpy(dtype=float) if not group.empty else np.empty((0, 3))
        padded = np.zeros((top_k, 3), dtype=float)
        if len(values):
            padded[: min(top_k, len(values)), :] = values[:top_k, :]
        for rank, (birth, death, persistence) in enumerate(padded, start=1):
            rows.append(
                {
                    "month_index": int(meta["month_index"]),
                    "month_label": month_label,
                    "year": int(meta["year"]),
                    "month": int(meta["month"]),
                    "rank": rank,
                    f"{prefix}_birth": float(birth),
                    f"{prefix}_death": float(death),
                    f"{prefix}_persistence": float(persistence),
                }
            )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def interval_summary(values: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    """Summarize a bootstrap distribution by mean, sd, and quantiles.

    Args:
        values: Bootstrap values.

    Returns:
        Summary series.
    """
    clean = values.astype(float).dropna()
    return pd.Series(
        {
            "bootstrap_mean": float(clean.mean()),
            "bootstrap_sd": float(clean.std(ddof=1)),
            "ci_2_5": float(clean.quantile(0.025)),
            "ci_50": float(clean.quantile(0.50)),
            "ci_97_5": float(clean.quantile(0.975)),
        }
    )


def summarize_ranked_points(frame: pd.DataFrame, prefix: str, group_cols: list[str]) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Summarize ranked persistence values within groups.

    Args:
        frame: Ranked birth-death table.
        prefix: Column prefix containing a `{prefix}_persistence` column.
        group_cols: Columns defining a group.

    Returns:
        Scalar summaries by group.
    """
    value_col = f"{prefix}_persistence"
    rows = []
    for key, group in frame.groupby(group_cols, dropna=False):
        if not isinstance(key, tuple):
            key = (key,)
        values = group[value_col].to_numpy(dtype=float)
        positive = values[values > 0]
        row = dict(zip(group_cols, key))
        row.update(
            {
                "n_positive_top10": int(len(positive)),
                "max_persistence_top10": float(values.max()) if len(values) else 0.0,
                "total_persistence_top10": float(values.sum()) if len(values) else 0.0,
                "mean_positive_persistence_top10": float(positive.mean()) if len(positive) else 0.0,
                "median_positive_persistence_top10": float(np.median(positive)) if len(positive) else 0.0,
            }
        )
        rows.append(row)
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def lambda1_on_grid(points: pd.DataFrame, birth_col: str, death_col: str, grid: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """Compute the first persistence landscape on a grid.

    Args:
        points: Birth-death point table.
        birth_col: Birth column name.
        death_col: Death column name.
        grid: Evaluation grid.

    Returns:
        First landscape values on the grid.
    """
    values = np.zeros((len(points), len(grid)), dtype=float)
    for row_index, (_, row) in enumerate(points.iterrows()):
        birth = float(row[birth_col])
        death = float(row[death_col])
        if not np.isfinite(birth) or not np.isfinite(death) or death <= birth:
            continue
        values[row_index, :] = np.maximum(np.minimum(grid - birth, death - grid), 0.0)
    return values.max(axis=0) if values.size else np.zeros(len(grid), dtype=float)


def average_monthly_lambda1(frame: pd.DataFrame, birth_col: str, death_col: str, grid: np.ndarray, group_cols: list[str]) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Compute average monthly first landscapes for one or more groups.

    Args:
        frame: Ranked birth-death point table.
        birth_col: Birth column name.
        death_col: Death column name.
        grid: Evaluation grid.
        group_cols: Optional grouping columns before month grouping.

    Returns:
        Long table with average lambda1 values by group and grid index.
    """
    rows = []
    grouped = frame.groupby(group_cols, dropna=False) if group_cols else [((), frame)]
    for group_key, group in grouped:
        if not isinstance(group_key, tuple):
            group_key = (group_key,)
        month_curves = [lambda1_on_grid(month_group, birth_col, death_col, grid) for _, month_group in group.groupby("month_label")]
        mean_curve = np.vstack(month_curves).mean(axis=0) if month_curves else np.zeros(len(grid), dtype=float)
        for grid_index, (x_value, lambda_value) in enumerate(zip(grid, mean_curve)):
            row = dict(zip(group_cols, group_key))
            row.update({"grid_index": grid_index, "x": float(x_value), "lambda1": float(lambda_value)})
            rows.append(row)
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def monthly_landscape_scalar(frame: pd.DataFrame, prefix: str, extra_group_cols: list[str] | None = None) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Compute simple landscape scalar summaries by month.

    Args:
        frame: Ranked birth-death points.
        prefix: Column prefix.
        extra_group_cols: Optional grouping columns, such as replicate.

    Returns:
        Landscape scalar summaries by month and optional extra groups.
    """
    persistence_col = f"{prefix}_persistence"
    group_cols = list(extra_group_cols or []) + ["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month"]
    rows = []
    for key, group in frame.groupby(group_cols, dropna=False):
        if not isinstance(key, tuple):
            key = (key,)
        persistence = group[persistence_col].to_numpy(dtype=float)
        persistence = persistence[persistence > 0]
        row = dict(zip(group_cols, key))
        row.update(
            {
                "landscape_lambda1_peak_top10": float(persistence.max() / 2.0) if len(persistence) else 0.0,
                "landscape_total_tent_area_top10": float(np.sum(persistence**2) / 4.0) if len(persistence) else 0.0,
            }
        )
        rows.append(row)
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


plane_replicates = pd.read_csv(SELECTED_MODEL_CACHE_DIR / "selected_model_plane_tube_bootstrap_replicates.csv")
plane_monthly = pd.read_csv(SELECTED_MODEL_CACHE_DIR / "selected_model_plane_tube_bootstrap_monthly.csv")
observed = pd.read_csv(SELECTED_MODEL_CACHE_DIR / "observed_h1_features.csv")
fitted = pd.read_csv(SELECTED_MODEL_CACHE_DIR / "selected_model_fitted_mean_field_h1_features.csv")
bootstrap_ranked_source = pd.read_csv(SELECTED_MODEL_CACHE_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_points_replicates.csv")
observed_ranked_source = top_ranked_from_features(observed, "observed", TOP_K)
fitted_ranked_source = top_ranked_from_features(fitted, "fitted", TOP_K)
observed_ranked_source.to_csv(SELECTED_MODEL_CACHE_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_points_observed.csv", index=False)
fitted_ranked_source.to_csv(SELECTED_MODEL_CACHE_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_points_fitted_mean.csv", index=False)

observed_summary = observed.groupby("month_label", as_index=False).agg(
    observed_n_h1=("persistence", "size"),
    observed_max_h1=("persistence", "max"),
    observed_total_h1=("persistence", "sum"),
)
fitted_summary = fitted.groupby("month_label", as_index=False).agg(
    fitted_n_h1=("persistence", "size"),
    fitted_max_h1=("persistence", "max"),
    fitted_total_h1=("persistence", "sum"),
)
observed_fitted_summary = month_meta.merge(observed_summary, on="month_label", how="left").merge(fitted_summary, on="month_label", how="left")
for column in ["observed_n_h1", "observed_max_h1", "observed_total_h1", "fitted_n_h1", "fitted_max_h1", "fitted_total_h1"]:
    observed_fitted_summary[column] = observed_fitted_summary[column].fillna(0)
observed_fitted_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_observed_fitted_h1_monthly_summary.csv", index=False)

old_max_persistence_diagnostic = q(plane_replicates["C_b"], 0.95)
global_bottleneck_radius = q(plane_replicates["R_b"], 0.95)
global_bottleneck_persistence_threshold = 2.0 * global_bottleneck_radius
monthwise_radius = plane_monthly.groupby(["month_index", "month_label"], as_index=False).agg(r95_t=("month_R_b", lambda x: q(x, 0.95)))
monthwise_radius["monthwise_bottleneck_persistence_threshold"] = 2.0 * monthwise_radius["r95_t"]
threshold_comparison = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"threshold_name": "old_max_simulated_persistence_global", "threshold": old_max_persistence_diagnostic, "n_months_above": 0},
        {"threshold_name": "new_global_bottleneck_perturbation", "threshold": global_bottleneck_persistence_threshold, "n_months_above": int((observed["persistence"] > global_bottleneck_persistence_threshold).groupby(observed["month_label"]).any().sum())},
        {"threshold_name": "monthwise_bottleneck_perturbation_sheet", "threshold": np.nan, "n_months_above": np.nan},
    ]
)
threshold_comparison.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_diagonal_plane_threshold_comparison.csv", index=False)

bottleneck_features = observed.merge(monthwise_radius, on=["month_index", "month_label"], how="left")
bottleneck_features["above_old_max_simulated_persistence_plane"] = bottleneck_features["persistence"] > old_max_persistence_diagnostic
bottleneck_features["significant_global_bottleneck_plane"] = bottleneck_features["persistence"] > global_bottleneck_persistence_threshold
bottleneck_features["significant_monthwise_bottleneck_sheet"] = bottleneck_features["persistence"] > bottleneck_features["monthwise_bottleneck_persistence_threshold"]

rank_delta = bootstrap_ranked_source.merge(
    fitted_ranked_source[["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank", "fitted_persistence"]],
    on=["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank"],
    how="left",
)
rank_delta["delta"] = (rank_delta["bootstrap_persistence"] - rank_delta["fitted_persistence"]).abs()
rank_monthwise = rank_delta.groupby(["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank"], as_index=False).agg(
    delta95_monthwise=("delta", lambda x: q(x, 0.95)),
    lower_pct_bootstrap_persistence=("bootstrap_persistence", lambda x: q(x, 0.025)),
    upper_pct_bootstrap_persistence=("bootstrap_persistence", lambda x: q(x, 0.975)),
)
rank_global = rank_delta.groupby(["replicate", "rank"], as_index=False).agg(delta_global_rank=("delta", "max"))
rank_global = rank_global.groupby("rank", as_index=False).agg(delta95_global_rank=("delta_global_rank", lambda x: q(x, 0.95)))
global_all = q(rank_delta.groupby("replicate")["delta"].max(), 0.95)
ranked_uncertainty = observed_ranked_source.merge(
    fitted_ranked_source[["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank", "fitted_persistence"]],
    on=["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank"],
    how="left",
).merge(rank_monthwise, on=["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank"], how="left").merge(rank_global, on="rank", how="left")
ranked_uncertainty["significant_monthwise_observed"] = ranked_uncertainty["observed_persistence"] > ranked_uncertainty["delta95_monthwise"]
ranked_uncertainty["significant_monthwise_fitted_mean"] = ranked_uncertainty["fitted_persistence"] > ranked_uncertainty["delta95_monthwise"]
ranked_uncertainty["significant_global_rank_observed"] = ranked_uncertainty["observed_persistence"] > ranked_uncertainty["delta95_global_rank"]
ranked_uncertainty["delta95_global_all_ranks"] = global_all
ranked_uncertainty["significant_global_all_ranks_observed"] = ranked_uncertainty["observed_persistence"] > global_all
ranked_uncertainty.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_top10_ranked_persistence_uncertainty.csv", index=False)

ranked_summary = ranked_uncertainty.groupby("rank", as_index=False).agg(
    months_tested=("month_index", "nunique"),
    months_significant_monthwise_observed=("significant_monthwise_observed", "sum"),
    months_significant_global_rank_observed=("significant_global_rank_observed", "sum"),
    months_significant_global_all_ranks_observed=("significant_global_all_ranks_observed", "sum"),
    max_observed_persistence=("observed_persistence", "max"),
    median_observed_persistence=("observed_persistence", "median"),
    median_delta95_monthwise=("delta95_monthwise", "median"),
    delta95_global_rank=("delta95_global_rank", "first"),
)
ranked_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_ranked_persistence_summary.csv", index=False)
top25_ranked = ranked_uncertainty.sort_values("observed_persistence", ascending=False).head(25).copy()
top25_ranked.insert(0, "overall_rank_by_observed_persistence", np.arange(1, len(top25_ranked) + 1))
top25_ranked.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_top25_ranked_persistence_features.csv", index=False)

ranked_month_flags = ranked_uncertainty.groupby(["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month"], as_index=False).agg(
    scalar_monthwise_any_rank=("significant_monthwise_observed", "any"),
    scalar_monthwise_n_significant_ranks=("significant_monthwise_observed", "sum"),
    scalar_global_rank_any_rank=("significant_global_rank_observed", "any"),
    scalar_global_rank_n_significant_ranks=("significant_global_rank_observed", "sum"),
    scalar_global_all_ranks_any_rank=("significant_global_all_ranks_observed", "any"),
    scalar_global_all_ranks_n_significant_ranks=("significant_global_all_ranks_observed", "sum"),
)
plane_month_flags = bottleneck_features.groupby(["month_index", "month_label"], as_index=False).agg(
    bottleneck_monthwise_sheet=("significant_monthwise_bottleneck_sheet", "any"),
    bottleneck_global_plane=("significant_global_bottleneck_plane", "any"),
    old_max_simulated_global_plane=("above_old_max_simulated_persistence_plane", "any"),
)
plane_month_summary = ranked_month_flags.merge(plane_month_flags, on=["month_index", "month_label"], how="left")
plane_month_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_plane_and_ranked_persistence_month_flags.csv", index=False)
plane_counts = pd.DataFrame([
    {"method": "bottleneck monthwise sheet", "n_months": int(plane_month_summary["bottleneck_monthwise_sheet"].sum())},
    {"method": "bottleneck global plane", "n_months": int(plane_month_summary["bottleneck_global_plane"].sum())},
    {"method": "scalar monthwise any rank", "n_months": int(plane_month_summary["scalar_monthwise_any_rank"].sum())},
    {"method": "scalar global rank any rank", "n_months": int(plane_month_summary["scalar_global_rank_any_rank"].sum())},
    {"method": "scalar global all ranks", "n_months": int(plane_month_summary["scalar_global_all_ranks_any_rank"].sum())},
])
plane_counts.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_plane_method_month_counts.csv", index=False)

tube_displacements = bootstrap_ranked_source.merge(
    fitted_ranked_source[["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank", "fitted_birth", "fitted_death", "fitted_persistence"]],
    on=["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank"],
    how="left",
)
tube_displacements["birth_abs_displacement"] = (tube_displacements["bootstrap_birth"] - tube_displacements["fitted_birth"]).abs()
tube_displacements["death_abs_displacement"] = (tube_displacements["bootstrap_death"] - tube_displacements["fitted_death"]).abs()
tube_displacements["birth_death_linf_displacement"] = tube_displacements[["birth_abs_displacement", "death_abs_displacement"]].max(axis=1)
tube_displacements.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_displacements.csv", index=False)
tube_monthwise = tube_displacements.groupby(["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank"], as_index=False).agg(
    radius_median_monthwise=("birth_death_linf_displacement", lambda x: q(x, 0.50)),
    radius_90_monthwise=("birth_death_linf_displacement", lambda x: q(x, 0.90)),
    radius_95_monthwise=("birth_death_linf_displacement", lambda x: q(x, 0.95)),
    radius_99_monthwise=("birth_death_linf_displacement", lambda x: q(x, 0.99)),
)
tube_monthwise = tube_monthwise.merge(observed_ranked_source, on=["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank"], how="left")
tube_monthwise = tube_monthwise.merge(fitted_ranked_source, on=["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "rank"], how="left")
tube_monthwise["observed_persistence_over_radius95"] = safe_ratio(tube_monthwise["observed_persistence"], tube_monthwise["radius_95_monthwise"])
tube_monthwise["fitted_persistence_over_radius95"] = safe_ratio(tube_monthwise["fitted_persistence"], tube_monthwise["radius_95_monthwise"])
tube_monthwise["observed_persistence_over_2radius95"] = safe_ratio(tube_monthwise["observed_persistence"], 2.0 * tube_monthwise["radius_95_monthwise"])
tube_monthwise["fitted_persistence_over_2radius95"] = safe_ratio(tube_monthwise["fitted_persistence"], 2.0 * tube_monthwise["radius_95_monthwise"])
tube_monthwise.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_radii_monthwise.csv", index=False)
global_rank = tube_displacements.groupby(["replicate", "rank"], as_index=False).agg(global_rank_radius=("birth_death_linf_displacement", "max"))
global_rank = global_rank.groupby("rank", as_index=False).agg(
    radius_90_global_rank=("global_rank_radius", lambda x: q(x, 0.90)),
    radius_95_global_rank=("global_rank_radius", lambda x: q(x, 0.95)),
    radius_99_global_rank=("global_rank_radius", lambda x: q(x, 0.99)),
).merge(
    observed_ranked_source.groupby("rank", as_index=False).agg(median_observed_persistence=("observed_persistence", "median"), max_observed_persistence=("observed_persistence", "max")),
    on="rank",
    how="left",
).merge(
    fitted_ranked_source.groupby("rank", as_index=False).agg(median_fitted_persistence=("fitted_persistence", "median"), max_fitted_persistence=("fitted_persistence", "max")),
    on="rank",
    how="left",
)
global_rank.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_radii_global_by_rank.csv", index=False)
global_all_source = tube_displacements.groupby("replicate")["birth_death_linf_displacement"].max()
tube_global_all = pd.DataFrame([{"radius_90_global_all_ranks": q(global_all_source, 0.90), "radius_95_global_all_ranks": q(global_all_source, 0.95), "radius_99_global_all_ranks": q(global_all_source, 0.99)}])
tube_global_all.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_radii_global_all_ranks.csv", index=False)
tube_top25 = tube_monthwise.merge(global_rank[["rank", "radius_95_global_rank"]], on="rank", how="left").sort_values("observed_persistence", ascending=False).head(25).copy()
tube_top25.insert(0, "overall_rank_by_observed_persistence", np.arange(1, len(tube_top25) + 1))
tube_top25.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_top25_observed_features.csv", index=False)
tube_summary = tube_monthwise.groupby("rank", as_index=False).agg(
    median_radius_95_monthwise=("radius_95_monthwise", "median"),
    max_radius_95_monthwise=("radius_95_monthwise", "max"),
    median_observed_persistence=("observed_persistence", "median"),
    max_observed_persistence=("observed_persistence", "max"),
    median_observed_persistence_over_2radius95=("observed_persistence_over_2radius95", "median"),
    n_months_observed_persistence_larger_than_2radius95=("observed_persistence_over_2radius95", lambda x: int((x > 1).sum())),
)
tube_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_rank_based_birth_death_tube_summary_by_rank.csv", index=False)

monthly_observed_scalar = summarize_ranked_points(observed_ranked_source, "observed", ["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month"])
monthly_fitted_scalar = summarize_ranked_points(fitted_ranked_source, "fitted", ["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month"])
monthly_bootstrap_scalar = summarize_ranked_points(bootstrap_ranked_source, "bootstrap", ["replicate", "month_index", "month_label", "year", "month"])
scalar_names = ["n_positive_top10", "max_persistence_top10", "total_persistence_top10", "mean_positive_persistence_top10", "median_positive_persistence_top10"]
monthly_interval_rows = []
for scalar in scalar_names:
    boot = monthly_bootstrap_scalar.groupby(["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month"])[scalar].apply(interval_summary).unstack().reset_index()
    boot["summary_statistic"] = scalar
    obs = monthly_observed_scalar[["month_index", "month_label", scalar]].rename(columns={scalar: "observed_value"})
    fit = monthly_fitted_scalar[["month_index", "month_label", scalar]].rename(columns={scalar: "fitted_mean_field_value"})
    monthly_interval_rows.append(boot.merge(obs, on=["month_index", "month_label"], how="left").merge(fit, on=["month_index", "month_label"], how="left"))
monthly_scalar_ci = pd.concat(monthly_interval_rows, ignore_index=True)
monthly_scalar_ci = monthly_scalar_ci[["month_index", "month_label", "year", "month", "summary_statistic", "observed_value", "fitted_mean_field_value", "bootstrap_mean", "bootstrap_sd", "ci_2_5", "ci_50", "ci_97_5"]]
monthly_scalar_ci.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "selected_model_monthly_scalar_summary_confidence_intervals.csv", index=False)
global_bootstrap_scalar = monthly_bootstrap_scalar.groupby("replicate", as_index=False).agg(
    total_positive_top10=("n_positive_top10", "sum"),
    max_persistence_top10=("max_persistence_top10", "max"),
    total_persistence_top10=("total_persistence_top10", "sum"),
```
